raw_phases: reader reads RM sticks + eps + scaler + gamma/beta

description: Full normalization pipeline: tilize, reduce_mean, sub_mean, square, reduce_var, add_eps+rsqrt, mul_inv_std, untilize. No gamma/beta.

approach: TensorAccessor for RM sticks, fill_with_val_bfloat16 for eps, calculate_and_prepare_reduce_scaler for scaler, gamma/beta read with face-layout scatter (guarded by has_gamma/has_beta constexpr)

8 phases: tilize, reduce_mean(WaitUpfrontNoPop), sub(COL,NoWaitNoPop/WaitAndPopPerTile), square(WaitUpfrontNoPop), reduce_var, add_eps+rsqrt(SCALAR,NoWaitNoPop for eps), mul_inv_std(COL,NoWaitNoPop/WaitAndPopPerTile), untilize. Manual pops for cb_tilized and cb_centered. Program-lifetime wait/pop for eps/gamma/beta.
TensorAccessor for output RM sticks, wait Wt tiles per tile-row, read 32 sticks from L1, noc_async_write each stick to DRAM, barrier + pop

cb_summary: c0:Wt/Wt c1:Wt/Wt(manual_pop) c2:1/1 c3:Wt/Wt(manual_pop) c4:Wt/Wt c5:1/1 c6:1/persist c7:1/1 c8:1/persist c24:Wt/Wt c16:Wt/Wt

details: reader: PoolType and ReduceDim not in scope - need ckernel:: or dataflow_kernel_lib:: namespace qualifiers

description: PoolType and ReduceDim are in ckernel:: namespace, not global scope. Need to use ckernel::PoolType::SUM and ckernel::ReduceDim::REDUCE_ROW or add using declarations
evidence: compiler error says PoolType not declared in scope, suggests ckernel::PoolType

change: Qualified PoolType and ReduceDim with ckernel:: namespace in calculate_and_prepare_reduce_scaler call

details: reader: TensorAccessorArgs has no member size(). Need to find proper way to get accessor arg count or hardcode the offsets.

description: TensorAccessorArgs uses next_compile_time_args_offset() to chain, not .size(). Fix gamma/beta accessor arg computation.
evidence: Found static constexpr next_compile_time_args_offset() in tensor_accessor_args.h

change: Replaced TensorAccessorArgs().size() with TensorAccessorArgs::next_compile_time_args_offset() for gamma and beta accessor arg chaining

details: reader: TensorAccessor constructor cant deduce DSpecT template param from TensorAccessorArgs<7>. Need to check how other kernels construct TensorAccessor.

description: TensorAccessorArgs<7> is instantiated even inside if constexpr(false) because template instantiation happens before constexpr evaluation. When has_gamma=0, compile-time arg at index 7 does not exist. Fix: use a safe dummy offset (e.g. 6) when has_gamma=0 to prevent invalid arg access.
evidence: Compile error at TensorAccessorArgs<7> when only 7 compile-time args exist (indices 0-6)

change: Rewrote reader to compute gamma_ct_offset outside if constexpr using next_compile_time_args_offset on input accessor. Gamma/beta TensorAccessorArgs only instantiated inside if constexpr blocks with valid offsets. Beta offset uses ternary with has_gamma.

details: TensorAccessorArgs<7> static member reads get_compile_time_arg_val(7) at class instantiation time even inside if constexpr. Fix: use safe fallback offset (input_ct_offset) when has_gamma=0 to avoid accessing non-existent args.

description: TensorAccessorArgs<N> class template reads get_compile_time_arg_val(N) in its static constexpr member at class instantiation time, regardless of if constexpr. Fix: use ternary to select input_ct_offset (safe) when has_gamma=0, so TensorAccessorArgs never reads a non-existent arg.
evidence: args_config static member in TensorAccessorArgs reads CTA_OFFSET at class definition

change: gamma_ct_offset now uses ternary: has_gamma ? next_offset : input_ct_offset. When has_gamma=0, TensorAccessorArgs<6> is instantiated (valid arg 6 exists). Same fix needed for beta_ct_offset_val.
beta_ct_offset_val also uses safe fallback to input_ct_offset when has_beta=0. Moved computation outside if constexpr block to ensure TensorAccessorArgs always instantiated with valid offset.

details: Max diff 2.73. Output looks like raw input values, not layer-normed. Normalization math not producing correct results. Possible cause: reduce scaler using SUM pooltype ignores reduce_factor and uses scaler 1.0 instead of 1/W.

description: calculate_and_prepare_reduce_scaler with SUM pooltype ignores reduce_factor and uses scaler=1.0. Need to use prepare_reduce_scaler with explicit 1.0f/W value, or use AVG pool type which applies 1/N scaler.
evidence: Docs say For SUM/MAX the reduce_factor is ignored and the scaler is 1.0. Output values match raw input suggesting mean was computed as sum (too large) causing normalization to be nearly identity.

change: Replaced calculate_and_prepare_reduce_scaler(SUM) with prepare_reduce_scaler(1.0f/W) to get correct 1/W scaler for mean computation

upstream_fixes: reduce scaler: replaced calculate_and_prepare_reduce_scaler(SUM) with prepare_reduce_scaler(1/W)
design_deviations: Used safe fallback offsets for TensorAccessorArgs when has_gamma/has_beta=0 to avoid compile-time arg access errors in non-template context

note: resuming from prior context - stage 1 already passed

description: Add gamma scale multiply. Reader reads gamma RM stick, scatters into tile face row 0. Compute multiplies normalized by gamma with ROW broadcast. Kernels already have if constexpr (has_gamma) paths from stage 1.

approach: No kernel changes needed - gamma paths (reader scatter, compute mul ROW broadcast, cb_untilize_src selection) already implemented in stage 1 via if constexpr guards

cb_summary: c0:Wt/Wt c1:Wt/Wt c2:1/1 c3:Wt/Wt c4:Wt/Wt c5:1/1 c6:1/persist c7:1/1 c8:1/persist c9:Wt/persist c24:Wt/Wt c25:Wt/Wt(untilize_src) c16:Wt/Wt

note: No kernel changes needed - gamma paths from stage 1 worked first try
Zero changes needed - gamma code paths implemented in stage 1 worked correctly
